source_jobs.title: Operations Coordinator - Second Shift
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Valerie Health is the AI front office for independent provider groups. We are reimagining how independent practices operate by using AI to modernize patient/provider communication, enhance care, and drive industry-changing operational efficiencies.

Why join us now?

 - Team: We are led by Pete Shalek https://www.linkedin.com/in/petershalek/ (2x health tech exits, including a $600M acquisition by Optum) and Nitin Joshi https://www.linkedin.com/in/njoshi023/ (scaled Uber Eats + Health, Stripe Connect, and Bridge–acquired by Stripe for $1.1B). Beyond our founders, our team brings DNA from category leaders (Uber, Stripe, Meta), high-growth unicorns, and top consulting firms.

 - Trajectory: We partner with leading independent provider groups across the U.S., automating hundreds of thousands of tasks each month. Scaling at 7x year-over-year with 300% NRR as customers consistently expand usage.

 - Funding: We have raised $39m from world-class investors including Redpoint Ventures, General Catalyst, Primary Ventures, and BoxGroup.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Our Operations team is the backbone of Valerie Health's healthcare platform, ensuring seamless data flow between our technology and partner medical practices.

As an Operations Coordinator, you’ll be part of our core frontline team responsible for verifying, processing, and routing clinical documents that impact real patients and real care. You’ll play a critical role in delivering timely, accurate, and high-quality work that helps medical practices run more efficiently, and ensures patients get the care they need.

We’re looking for individuals who take pride in doing great work and want to grow within a fast-moving, mission-driven team. This role offers clear advancement opportunities as you deepen proficiency in our workflows that drive the company’s growth and operational effectiveness.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Support Provider Practices: Process and verify patient and referral data with a high degree of accuracy and urgency using internal and our partners’ tools.

 - Maintain Operational Quality: Follow standard operating procedures while also surfacing bugs or patterns that impact quality or speed.

 - Ensure Timely Turnaround: Meet internal SLAs for task completion to ensure partner practices and patients aren’t left waiting.

 - Collaborate Across Teams: Provide feedback to Engineering and Product teams to improve tooling and automation.

 - Continuously Improve: Participate in training and calibration to ensure ongoing quality and performance.

 


ABOUT YOU

Experience:

 - A high school diploma or equivalent required; associate’s or bachelor’s degree preferred

 - 0-1+ years of experience in administrative support, medical data entry, customer service, or operations in a process-driven environment

 - Familiarity with EHRs or healthcare workflows is a plus

Attributes:

 - Detail-Oriented: You’re meticulous, accurate, and understand how small errors can have big consequences

 - Dependable & Consistent: You take pride in doing your job well every day and helping others do the same

 - Operationally Minded: You enjoy executing clear workflows and solving problems with repeatable systems

 - Mission-Driven: You want to make healthcare better and believe that great operations make it possible

 - Team-Oriented: You thrive in a collaborative environment and enjoy being part of a shared mission

 


COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Compensation: $20/hour

 - Hours: This shift will be 1:30pm - 10:00pm EST

 - Benefits: (Almost) fully covered medical, dental, vision, and 401(k)

 - Equity: Ownership in a high-growth, AI-native startup

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Valerie Health is the AI front office for independent provider groups. We are reimagining how independent practices operate by using AI to modernize patient/provider communication, enhance care, and drive industry-changing operational efficiencies.

Why join us now?

 - Team: We are led by Pete Shalek https://www.linkedin.com/in/petershalek/ (2x health tech exits, including a $600M acquisition by Optum) and Nitin Joshi https://www.linkedin.com/in/njoshi023/ (scaled Uber Eats + Health, Stripe Connect, and Bridge–acquired by Stripe for $1.1B). Beyond our Founders, our team brings decades of experience from Stripe, Uber, and Meta, combining deep expertise in ML systems, large-scale infrastructure, and product design.

 - Trajectory: We partner with leading independent provider groups across the U.S., automating hundreds of thousands of tasks each month. Scaling at 7x year-over-year with 300% NRR as customers consistently expand usage.

 - Funding: We have raised $39m from world-class investors including Redpoint Ventures, General Catalyst, Primary Ventures, and BoxGroup.

Our technical stack combines:

 - Vision models to interpret unstructured healthcare data and build a deep clinical ontology

 - Language and speech models for automated, empathetic text and voice communication with patients

 - LLM-based workflows that integrate directly with EMRs

 - RPA and desktop automation to bridge fragmented and legacy systems
   

About the role

As a founding engineer, you will get a front seat into building a world-class startup. You will help shape the technical foundation of Valerie Health, and drive both our product and business from inception to scale. You will play a lead role in designing and implementing a robust backend infrastructure, building AI models across various modalities and use-cases (OCR, Voice, LLM, RPA) with RLHF, and creating delightful user experiences. 


What you’ll do

 - Collaborate closely with the rest of the team to shape product, business, and company direction. Help define and shape the culture of the company.

 - Build a production-ready AI infrastructure, integrating and fine-tuning models across voice, text, and desktop agents. Build a reinforcement learning system with internal and external humans in the loop. 

 - Own end to end product surface areas that serve external and internal users. Build deeply empathetic and polished user experiences that raise the bar across the industry. 

 - Design, build and scale a HIPAA-compliant backend infrastructure that’s highly secure with strong availability and performance. 


About you

 - Experience:
   
   - 1 - 10+ years of experience in software engineering, with a strong background in computer science.
   
   - Previous experience in big tech or startups is a plus.
   
   - Demonstrated experience in building end-to-end products.

 - Attributes:
   
   - User-Focus: Deep empathy for users, with the ability to translate their needs into product features and prioritize effectively.
   
   - Work as Craft: Interest in producing elegant, polished, and high-performance work, finding pride and joy in the details.
   
   - Growth Mindset: Eagerness to learn new approaches, seek feedback, and continually improve skill sets.
   
   - Strong Communicator: Clear and succinct in both written and verbal communication, capable of adjusting style for different audiences and conveying technical topics to non-technical stakeholders.
   
   - Self-Starter & Adaptable: Proactively own projects from inception to completion, identify challenges, and offer solutions, thriving in a dynamic work environment.

Details

 - Competitive salary, equity and benefits including medical, dental, vision, 401(k) and PTO

 - Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status

 - S

source_jobs.title: AI Operations Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Valerie Health is the AI front office for independent provider groups. We are reimagining how independent practices operate by using AI to modernize patient and provider communication, enhance care, and drive industry changing operational efficiencies.





WHY JOIN US NOW?

Team: We are led by Pete Shalek https://www.linkedin.com/in/petershalek/ (2x health tech exits, including a $600M acquisition by Optum) and Nitin Joshi https://www.linkedin.com/in/njoshi023/ (scaled Uber Eats + Health, Stripe Connect, and Bridge, acquired by Stripe for $1.1B). Beyond our founders, our team brings DNA from category leaders (Uber, Stripe, Meta), high growth unicorns, and top consulting firms.

Trajectory: We partner with leading independent provider groups across the U.S., automating hundreds of thousands of tasks each month. Scaling at 7x year-over-year with 300% NRR as customers consistently expand usage.

Funding: We have raised $39M from world class investors including Redpoint Ventures, General Catalyst, Primary Ventures, and BoxGroup.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Operations Specialists are the operators behind Valerie’s AI platform. Each specialist owns the operational delivery for a set of partner medical groups, ensuring their workflows run accurately, efficiently, and reliably.

You will serve as the primary operational owner (Ops POC) for assigned customers, responsible for maintaining high-quality execution while helping improve the systems and workflows that power Valerie’s platform.

For high-performing team members, this role offers meaningful growth opportunities within the first 6-12 months. As Valerie scales, strong operators often expand their scope beyond core workflow execution to drive process improvements, support automation initiatives, and help shape how operations runs across new partners and products.

Working inside a fast-growing AI-native company also provides exposure to how different parts of the business operate, including operations, engineering, and customer teams. This environment creates opportunities for strong performers to grow into new roles within Valerie over time, while developing skills that translate well across the broader startup ecosystem.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own Operational Delivery for Customers: Serve as the primary operations POC for partner practices, ensuring their workflows run smoothly and meet quality and performance expectations, including supporting the rollout and implementation of new product lines.

 - Execute Core Platform Workflows: Process and verify patient and practice data through Valerie’s internal systems with high accuracy and speed.

 - Maintain Quality and Data Integrity: Identify discrepancies, edge cases, and workflow issues and resolve or escalate them quickly to maintain platform reliability.

 - Improve Operational Systems: Identify opportunities to reduce manual work, improve workflow efficiency, and strengthen operational processes.

 - Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Work closely with Central Operations, Engineering, and Customer teams to surface issues, provide operational insights, and help improve the platform.




OWN YOUR IMPACT

We move fast and build big. We're scaling rapidly, solving real problems for providers, and we work with craft. You'll own your work end to end, with the autonomy to make the decisions that matter. We hire exceptional people, hold them to a high standard, and the professional growth that follows is significant. The pace is demanding and the work is intense. It's also where the best people do their defining work. If that resonates with you, let's talk.




ABOUT YOU

Experience:

 - Bachelor’s degree with a strong academic track record or equivalent experience with a trajectory of high achievement.

 - Early-career candidates are encouraged to apply. Prior startup, operations, or healthcare experience is helpful but not required.

 - Demonstrated ability to learn new systems, tools, and workflows quickly.

Attributes:

source_jobs.title: Customer Strategy
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Valerie Health is the AI front office for independent provider groups. We are reimagining how independent practices operate by using AI to modernize patient/provider communication, enhance care, and drive industry-changing operational efficiencies.

 - Team: We are led by Pete Shalek https://www.linkedin.com/in/petershalek/ (2x health tech exits, including a $600M acquisition by Optum) and Nitin Joshi https://www.linkedin.com/in/njoshi023/ (scaled Uber Eats + Health, Stripe Connect, and Bridge, acquired by Stripe for $1.1B). Beyond our founders, our team brings DNA from category leaders (Uber, Stripe, Meta), high-growth unicorns, and top consulting firms.

 - Trajectory: We partner with leading independent provider groups across the U.S., automating hundreds of thousands of tasks each month. Scaling at 7x year-over-year with 300% NRR as customers consistently expand usage.

 - Funding: We have raised $39M (Series A) from world-class investors including Redpoint Ventures, General Catalyst, Primary Ventures, and BoxGroup.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Reporting to the Head of Customers, this role will drive our next tier of growth with some of our largest customers. You will be responsible for going deep with our partners, in both implementation and account growth, understanding how we deliver maximum value now and become core to their operations.

This is an inherently cross-functional role, requiring technical fluency, customer chops and high levels of ownership. You will be the primary point of contact for your customers and need to independently manage those relationships, driving product adoption and identifying opportunities for growth.

The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of the business and operations sides of healthcare, enabling you to be a trusted partner to our customers and understanding the nuances of practice operations and success. You are equally comfortable jumping into data, talking a customer through implementation tradeoffs and partnering on strategic vision with the exec team. This is the opportunity engine of the business, and you’ll work closely with operations, engineering and go-to-market to ensure success of launches, delivery and growth.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Partner Deeply: Serve as the lead for a set of provider customers, being the primary point of contact in the customer relationship and developing a deep understanding of their business goals and operational workflows.

 - Identify New Product and Growth Opportunities: Dive into what matters most to our customers and identify, define and drive product opportunities to improve and deliver maximum value.

 - Own a Customer End-to-End: Engage during the sales process, implement new customers, monitor happiness, review progress and data outcomes, and strategically identify opportunities for expansion and upsell to help us do more for our customers.

 - Create Data-Driven Solutions: Identify, respond to and recommend solutions to any customer issues by digging into data and collaborating with internal teams (e.g. Ops, Engineering) to ensure continued improvement and success.

 - Build the Playbook: Contribute to the development of best practices and playbooks for the Customer Strategy team, defining how we support and grow our customers quarter over quarter.




ABOUT YOU


EXPERIENCE:

 - 3+ years of experience in high-growth environments or ambiguity-heavy roles (e.g. startups, consulting, VC/PE/IB)

 - Track record of expansively owning and driving key business outcomes on high-performing teams

 - Strong tech toolkit: advanced SQL and spreadsheet skills (data cleaning, analysis, and modeling) or code fluency


ATTRIBUTES:

 - Problem Solver: You enjoy identifying problems and designing solutions, whether process or product, and bring structure to unstructured problems to drive execution and scale.

 - Ownership Mentality: You take full responsibility for outcomes, follow through without being asked, and hold yourself to a h

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Valerie Health is the AI front office for independent provider groups. We are reimagining how independent practices operate by using AI to modernize patient/provider communication, enhance care, and drive industry-changing operational efficiencies.

Why join us now?

 - Team: We are led by Pete Shalek https://www.linkedin.com/in/petershalek/ (2x health tech exits, including a $600M acquisition by Optum) and Nitin Joshi https://www.linkedin.com/in/njoshi023/ (scaled Uber Eats + Health, Stripe Connect, and Bridge–acquired by Stripe for $1.1B). Beyond our founders, our team brings DNA from category leaders (Uber, Stripe, Meta), high-growth unicorns, and top consulting firms.

 - Trajectory: We partner with leading independent provider groups across the U.S., automating hundreds of thousands of tasks each month. Scaling at 7x year-over-year with 300% NRR as customers consistently expand usage.

 - Funding: We have raised $39m from world-class investors including Redpoint Ventures, General Catalyst, Primary Ventures, and BoxGroup.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Account Executive at Valerie Health, you'll be at the forefront of our growth, bringing transformative AI-powered solutions to independent practices across the country. You'll own the full sales cycle—from prospecting and qualification to demo, negotiation, and close—while working closely with our Customer Strategy team to ensure seamless handoffs and long-term customer satisfaction and growth.

The ideal candidate has a proven track record in healthcare technology sales, understands the operational challenges facing independent practices, and thrives in fast-paced, high-growth startup environments. You'll be joining at an inflection point where your impact will directly shape our go-to-market strategy and revenue trajectory.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Drive New Business: Own and exceed quarterly revenue targets by building pipeline, running discovery calls, conducting product demonstrations, and closing deals with independent practices.

 - Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Partner closely with our Customers and Operations team to ensure smooth onboarding, with Product to communicate customer feedback, and with Marketing to refine messaging and positioning.

 - Refine the Playbook: Contribute to the development of our sales methodology, ideal customer profiles, and go-to-market strategies as we scale from early-stage startup to market leader.

 - Build Strategic Relationships: Cultivate ongoing relationships with key stakeholders in target practices, including practice managers, medical directors, and C-suite executives, to navigate complex buying processes and upsells.
   


ABOUT YOU

 - Experience:
   
   - 5+ years of B2B sales experience at a high-growth company, bonus for experience selling in healthcare
   
   - Proven history of closing six- to seven-figure deals ($100k-$1M+ ACV)
   
   - Proven track record of exceeding quota in a fast-paced, high-growth environment
   
   - Experience selling to healthcare providers, practice administrators, or PE backed provider groups stakeholders strongly preferred

 - Attributes:
   
   - Hunter Mentality: You're energized by building pipeline, identifying new opportunities, and opening doors with prospective customers through creative outreach.
   
   - Healthcare Passion: You are motivated by the opportunity to help providers deliver better care and run better businesses.
   
   - Consultative Approach: You lead with curiosity and empathy, asking thoughtful questions to uncover pain points and positioning solutions based on customer needs rather than product features.
   
   - Resilience & Grit: You embrace rejection as part of the process, learn quickly from setbacks, and maintain high energy and optimism through the inevitable ups and downs of sales.
   
   - Clear Communicator: You articulate complex technical concepts in simple, compelling terms and tailor your message to resonate with different audiences, whethe

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Valerie Health is the AI front office for independent provider groups. We are reimagining how independent practices operate by using AI to modernize patient/provider communication, enhance care, and drive industry-changing operational efficiencies.

 - Team: We are led by Pete Shalek https://www.linkedin.com/in/petershalek/ (2x health tech exits, including a $600M acquisition by Optum) and Nitin Joshi https://www.linkedin.com/in/njoshi023/ (scaled Uber Eats + Health, Stripe Connect, and Bridge, acquired by Stripe for $1.1B). Beyond our founders, our team brings DNA from category leaders (Uber, Stripe, Meta), high-growth unicorns, and top consulting firms.

 - Trajectory: We partner with leading independent provider groups across the U.S., automating hundreds of thousands of tasks each month. Scaling at 7x year-over-year with 300% NRR as customers consistently expand usage.

 - Funding: We have raised $39M (Series A) from world-class investors including Redpoint Ventures, General Catalyst, Primary Ventures, and BoxGroup.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Valerie Health is in a hypergrowth phase, and we are rapidly building the marketing function from the ground up. You will be one of the first hires, working with high autonomy and responsibility.

AI is transforming healthcare in ways that were impossible with traditional software: problems that have been intractable for decades are now being solved. This is a chance to define how that story gets told, shaping the positioning and competitive narrative for a company at the center of it.

As our Product Marketing Manager, you will own Valerie’s story. You will develop the messaging, positioning, and collateral that make our value clear to every person we touch. You will develop consistent messaging frameworks across buyer personas and product categories, work with sales on pricing strategy and competitive positioning, and create high-quality collateral with external partners or building yourself.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Messaging Frameworks. Develop messaging for Valerie's product suite, tailored to different buyer personas (practice administrators, C-suite, PE investors) and different product categories.

 - Competitive Positioning. Understand how Valerie fits in the market and arm sales with the language, battle cards, and materials to win.

 - Pricing Strategy. Work with sales leadership on pricing and the positioning of bundled vs. standalone product offerings.

 - Collateral. Create and manage production of sales decks, one-pagers, case studies, conference materials, and website copy. You should have a strong eye for design and the ability to work in Figma and direct an external partner.

 - Product Launches. Own go-to-market strategy for new product launches and feature releases: announcement copy, updated collateral, and sales talking points.

 - Sales Enablement. Build materials that help reps articulate Valerie's value clearly and consistently.

 - Brand Consistency. Ensure consistency of messaging across all external-facing communications.

 - Cross-Team Alignment. Partner with Growth, Brand, and Sales to ensure campaigns, outreach, and conference collateral are aligned with positioning.




ABOUT YOU

You care about craft. You're the kind of person who rewrites a headline six times because the first five didn't earn the click. You have done this before — built messaging frameworks, created collateral that sales actually uses, and worked on positioning that drove business success — and you can show us the work.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3–5+ years of product marketing experience. B2B SaaS, enterprise software, or healthcare technology strongly preferred.

 - Proven ability to develop messaging and positioning from scratch. You can take the complex and make it a clear and compelling story for a specific audience.

 - Strong collateral creation skills. You build high-quality decks, one-pagers, and case studies. You have a good eye for design and can work in Figma

source_jobs.title: Head of Talent Acquisition
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Valerie Health is the AI front office for independent provider groups. We are reimagining how independent practices operate by using AI to modernize patient/provider communication, enhance care, and drive industry-changing operational efficiencies.

Why join us now?

 - Team: We are led by Pete Shalek https://www.linkedin.com/in/petershalek/ (2x health tech exits, including a $600M acquisition by Optum) and Nitin Joshi https://www.linkedin.com/in/njoshi023/ (scaled Uber Eats + Health, Stripe Connect, and Bridge–acquired by Stripe for $1.1B). Beyond our founders, our team brings DNA from category leaders (Uber, Stripe, Meta), high-growth unicorns, and top consulting firms.

 - Trajectory: We partner with leading independent provider groups across the U.S., automating hundreds of thousands of tasks each month. Scaling at 7x year-over-year with 300% NRR as customers consistently expand usage.

 - Funding: We have raised $39m from world-class investors including Redpoint Ventures, General Catalyst, Primary Ventures, and BoxGroup.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a strategic, hands-on Head of Talent Acquisition to build and scale the talent function at Valerie Health. You will partner directly with the founders and leadership team to shape the company through hiring, org design, talent strategy, and recruiting operations.

You will personally lead searches for critical leadership, technical, and strategic hires while also building the systems, processes, and team needed to scale recruiting across the company. This role combines high-touch executive and strategic recruiting with operational leadership and talent strategy.

This is not a traditional recruiting role. You will help define how Valerie attracts talent, raises the hiring bar, develops managers, and scales culture through a period of hypergrowth.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Executive & Strategic Hiring: You will personally own and close critical leadership, technical, and strategic hires across the company. You will operate as a high-judgment talent partner to founders and hiring managers, helping define role scopes, calibrate talent bars, and drive hiring decisions.

 - Talent Strategy: You will partner with leadership on org design, leveling, compensation benchmarking, hiring plans, and workforce strategy as the company scales.

 - Recruiting Leadership: You will oversee and scale the recruiting function, including recruiting operations, external recruiting partners, and high-volume hiring across delivery and operations teams. You will coach recruiters and hiring managers to improve hiring quality, calibration, and consistency.

 - Talent Branding: You will help shape Valerie’s talent brand and position the company as a destination for ambitious operators, engineers, and leaders. You will drive outbound sourcing, referrals, and executive-level candidate engagement.

 - Recruiting Operations: You will build scalable recruiting infrastructure, including ATS workflows, interview design, funnel analytics, calibration processes, and reporting systems that enable disciplined hiring at scale.

 - Candidate Experience: You will define what it feels like to interview at Valerie Health. Every candidate interaction should reflect a high-talent-density, mission-driven, high-performance culture.
   
   


WHO YOU ARE

 - Experience: You have 4-8+ years of recruiting and talent experience across high-growth startups, talent-dense technology companies, and/or top-tier search firms. You have experience hiring senior technical and business leaders and ideally have built or scaled recruiting functions from an early stage.

 - Strategic Operator: You operate comfortably at both the strategic and execution levels. You can shape hiring strategy while building scalable processes, strong funnel visibility, and operational rigor across recruiting. Experience with Ashby is a strong plus.

 - Talent Bar: You have strong calibration and high standards. You understand how to ide

source_jobs.title: Agent Operations
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Valerie Health is the AI front office for independent provider groups. We are reimagining how independent practices operate by using AI to modernize patient/provider communication, enhance care, and drive industry-changing operational efficiencies.

 - Team: We are led by Pete Shalek https://www.linkedin.com/in/petershalek/ (2x health tech exits, including a $600M acquisition by Optum) and Nitin Joshi https://www.linkedin.com/in/njoshi023/ (scaled Uber Eats + Health, Stripe Connect, and Bridge, acquired by Stripe for $1.1B). Beyond our founders, our team brings DNA from category leaders (Uber, Stripe, Meta), high-growth unicorns, and top consulting firms.

 - Trajectory: We partner with leading independent provider groups across the U.S., automating hundreds of thousands of tasks each month. Scaling at 7x year-over-year with 300% NRR as customers consistently expand usage.

 - Funding: We have raised $39M (Series A) from world-class investors including Redpoint Ventures, General Catalyst, Primary Ventures, and BoxGroup.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Reporting to the Head of Customers, this role owns the implementation, deployment and performance of Valerie's AI voice agents across our customers. You will be responsible for going deep with our customers in implementation to understand their clinical and operational workflows and tuning our agents to ensure we deliver on our goals.

This is a hands-on, cross-functional role. You need to be technically fluent, comfortable in direct customer relationships, and willing to own things end to end. You'll work closely with Customer Strategy and Engineering to close the loop between what customers need and the way our agents operate.

The ideal candidate is someone who tinkers until something works exactly right and likes the challenge of complex puzzles. You have high standards, low ego, and a bias toward figuring things out. You're equally comfortable digging into logs to debug a problem and sitting across from a practice manager explaining why something isn't working yet.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own Deployments End-to-End: Lead voice agent implementations from discovery through go-live. Scope workflows, configure agents, run tests, and stay accountable for performance post-launch.

 - Tune and Improve: Monitor how our agents are performing, through conversation review and eval testing, and drive improvements with engineering

 - Uncover Gaps through Data: Dive deep in our data, setting up reporting and investigating aberrations to improve our product performance, reliability and effectiveness.

 - Identify Product Expansion Opportunities: Spot where voice can be improved, through new functionality or capabilities, based on deep knowledge of our technology and customer insights.

 - Build the Playbook: Tune our process, not just our agents. Turn one-off solutions into repeatable processes so the team scales without losing quality.

 


ABOUT YOU


EXPERIENCE:

 - Previous experience in consulting, implementation, technical account management, solutions engineering, or a comparable analytical + technical customer-facing roles.

 - Technical background: STEM degree or equivalent experience strongly preferred

 - Experience in high-growth or ambiguity-heavy environments (startups, consulting) a strong plus.
   


ATTRIBUTES:

 - Problem Solver: You enjoy identifying problems and designing solutions, whether process or product, and bring structure to unstructured problems to drive execution and scale.

 - Ownership Mentality: You take full responsibility for outcomes, follow through without being asked, and hold yourself to a high bar.

 - Technical Fluency: You can learn systems quickly, work through configuration without hand-holding, and speak credibly with both customers and engineering teams.

 - Strong Communicator: You are clear and succinct in both written and verbal communication, capable of adjusting style for different audiences.

 - Growth Mindset: You are eager to le

source_jobs.title: Central Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Valerie Health is the AI front office for independent provider groups. We are reimagining how independent practices operate by using AI to modernize patient/provider communication, enhance care, and drive industry-changing operational efficiencies.

Why join us now?

 - Team: We are led by Pete Shalek https://www.linkedin.com/in/petershalek/ (2x health tech exits, including a $600M acquisition by Optum) and Nitin Joshi https://www.linkedin.com/in/njoshi023/ (scaled Uber Eats + Health, Stripe Connect, and Bridge–acquired by Stripe for $1.1B). Beyond our founders, our team brings DNA from category leaders (Uber, Stripe, Meta), high-growth unicorns, and top consulting firms.

 - Trajectory: We partner with leading independent provider groups across the U.S., automating hundreds of thousands of tasks each month. Scaling at 7x year-over-year with 300% NRR as customers consistently expand usage.

 - Funding: We have raised $39m from world-class investors including Redpoint Ventures, General Catalyst, Primary Ventures, and BoxGroup.




ABOUT THE ROLE

The Central Operations team builds the systems that enable Valerie’s operations to scale. The team is responsible for workforce strategy, capacity planning, quality measurement, enablement, and operating model design.

Reporting into the Head of Operations, this role is responsible for designing the operating models and allocation frameworks that determine how work is distributed across Valerie's delivery system. You will help shape how work is allocated across internal teams, automation systems, and outsourced operations partners, while owning Valerie’s outsourced operations infrastructure.

You will help answer questions such as:

 - Which workflows should remain internal versus externalized, and how should work be segmented based on complexity, quality sensitivity, or customer impact?

 - How should operational allocation models evolve as workflow complexity and customer volume grow?

 - Where and how should we use pull vs push-based task assignment models?

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys combining systems thinking, analytics, and operational execution to solve complex scaling challenges.

Leveling is flexible. Exceptional candidates may be considered for a broader scope and level based on their experience and demonstrated impact.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Design Workflow Allocation Systems: Build the operational systems and allocation logic that determine how work is segmented, prioritized, and distributed across internal teams and external operational capacity.

 - Design Scalable Operating Models. Develop scalable workforce models and operational processes that improve throughput, quality, and operational efficiency as workflow complexity and customer volume grow.

 - Managed Outsourced Operations. Own Valerie’s outsourced operations infrastructure, including partner onboarding, quality management, performance monitoring, escalations, and operational reviews.

 - Partner Cross-Functionally. Work closely with stakeholders across Operations, Engineering, and Customer Strategy teams to improve operational scalability and support customer growth.


ABOUT YOU

Experience:

 - 3-5+ years of experience in marketplace operations, business operations, consulting, workforce planning, or vendor management, preferably in a high-growth startup environment

 - Strong analytical toolkit, including proficiency in SQL and experience using operational data to drive decisions

 - Experience working with outsourced operations, BPOs, or distributed workforce models is a plus

 - Experience working cross-functionally with technical and operational stakeholders

Attributes:

 - Systems Thinker. You naturally think in workflows, operating models, and scalable systems rather than one-off fixes.

 - Structured and Analytical. You use frameworks and data to make decisions, prioritize tradeoffs, and solve ambiguous operational problems.

 - Strong Operator. You move comfortably bet

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
About the company

Valerie Health is the AI front office for independent provider groups. We are reimagining how independent practices operate by using AI to modernize patient/provider communication, enhance care, and drive industry-changing operational efficiencies.

Why join us now?

 - Team: We are led by Pete Shalek (2x health tech exits, including a $600M acquisition by Optum) and Nitin Joshi (scaled Uber Eats + Health, Stripe Connect, and Bridge-acquired by Stripe for $1.1B). Beyond our founders, our team brings DNA from category leaders (Uber, Stripe, Meta), high-growth unicorns, and top consulting firms.

 - Trajectory: We partner with leading independent provider groups across the U.S., automating hundreds of thousands of tasks each month. Scaling at 7x year-over-year with 300% NRR as customers consistently expand usage.

 - Funding: We have raised $39M (Series A) from world-class investors including Redpoint Ventures, General Catalyst, Primary Ventures, and BoxGroup.

About the role

As a Business Development Representative at Valerie Health, you'll be the first point of contact for the independent provider groups we're serving. Your job is to open doors: identify the right prospects, reach them with messaging that actually resonates, and set the stage for our account executives to close.

This isn't a role where you read from a script. Our buyers – practice administrators, COOs, VPs of Operations – are smart, busy, and skeptical. You'll need to understand their world well enough to earn their time, not just request it.

You'll work closely with our GTM team in a fast-moving environment where your ideas, your patterns, and your wins directly shape how we grow.

What you’ll do

 - Own top-of-funnel prospecting: research, prioritize, and contact leads

 - Execute personalized, multi-channel outreach (email, phone, LinkedIn, in person visits) that speaks directly to the operational challenges practices face

 - Qualify inbound leads and route them efficiently to account executives

 - Build and maintain pipeline in HubSpot, keeping records clean and stages accurate

 - Partner with the GTM team to refine ICP targeting, test new messaging, and iterate on sequences

 - Represent Valerie Health at industry conferences and virtual events

 - Share what you're hearing in the field (competitor intel, objections, pain points) to sharpen how we sell

About you

Experience:

 - 1-2 years of experience in a sales, BDR/SDR, or customer-facing role (B2B preferred)

 - Proficiency with CRM tools (HubSpot experience a plus)

 - Prior experience at a high-growth startup

 - Nice-to-have: Exposure to AI or workflow automation products, healthcare familiarity

Attributes:

 - Incredibly Curious: Genuine curiosity about healthcare operations and a desire to understand the problems our customers are solving. You bring warmth and are curious about people.

 - Self-Starter & Adaptable: Comfort working in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment where priorities shift and you're expected to figure things out

 - Strong Communicator: Clear, confident written and verbal communication

 - Growth Mindset: Eagerness to learn new approaches, seek feedback, and continually improve skill sets.

 - Action Oriented: Bias to getting things done

Details

 - The compensation range for this position is between $85,000–$110,000k OTE annually + equity and benefits

 - Medical, dental, and vision coverage

 - Flexible PTO

 - Hybrid work environment; Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday in office

 - San Francisco: Daily lunch provided in the office and dinner after 7pm

 - Opportunity to grow into an AE or other GTM role as the team expands

source_jobs.title: Operations Team Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Valerie Health is the AI front office for independent provider groups. We are reimagining how independent practices operate by using AI to modernize patient and provider communication, enhance care, and drive industry changing operational efficiencies.




WHY JOIN US NOW?

Team: We are led by Pete Shalek (2x health tech exits, including a $600M acquisition by Optum) and Nitin Joshi (scaled Uber Eats + Health, Stripe Connect, and Bridge, acquired by Stripe for $1.1B). Beyond our founders, our team brings DNA from category leaders (Uber, Stripe, Meta), high growth unicorns, and top consulting firms.

Trajectory: We partner with leading independent specialty groups across the U.S., automating hundreds of thousands of tasks each month and growing 50%+ month over month with 300% net revenue retention.

Funding: We have raised $39M from world class investors including Redpoint Ventures, General Catalyst, Primary Ventures, and BoxGroup.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for an Operations Team Lead to manage a group of Ops Coordinators while also contributing directly to customer delivery work and strategic projects.

In this role, you will lead a team responsible for delivering work to customers while remaining close to the day-to-day operational workflows. You will focus on developing people, ensuring reliable delivery from your team, and maintaining ownership of a high vis/complex customer. This role combines people leadership and hands-on operational ownership. You will coach and develop operators, ensure clear expectations and accountability, and support customer success by directly contributing to delivery in a meaningful way.

You will report to the Director of Operations and partner closely with Operations leadership as well as cross-functionally with Engineering and Customer teams. This is a leadership role for someone who can both develop strong operators and contribute meaningfully to delivery in a fast-moving operational environment.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Lead and Develop Your Team: Directly manage a group of Ops Coordinators, providing coaching, performance feedback, and development opportunities. Set clear expectations, reinforce operational standards, and address performance gaps early to ensure consistent delivery.

 - Delivery Ownership: Serve as the POC (Point of Contact) for select customers and/or own complex delivery projects and operational initiatives, ensuring work is executed accurately and on time.

 - Support Live Operations: Help stabilize delivery during high-volume periods by prioritizing work, resolving operational issues, and stepping in when necessary to support the team and maintain continuity.

 - Proactively Identify Risks: Stay close to day-to-day delivery and team dynamics, identifying risks to delivery quality, team health, or workflow performance early and intervening or escalating to leadership when needed.

 - Support Scheduling and Coverage: Partner with the owner of live ops on shift scheduling and workload distribution to ensure balanced coverage across customers and workflows. Help identify and fill coverage gaps as needed to maintain delivery continuity and meet operational demand.


ABOUT YOU




EXPERIENCE

 - 3+ years of experience in operations, delivery, or service environments.

 - Experience working in fast-paced environments where delivery quality and responsiveness matter.

 - Proven experience managing, coaching, and developing team members within an operational team.

 - Comfortable working within operational systems and workflows, with experience using dashboards, spreadsheets, and performance metrics.


ATTRIBUTES

 - Operational Focus: You stay close to day-to-day delivery and operate effectively in a fast-paced environment. You help keep work moving, adapt as priorities shift, and step in to support the team when volume or complexity increases.

 - Ownership Mentality: You take responsibility for the quality and completion of work across your scope. Whe

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Product
source_jobs.description:
Valerie Health builds the ML infrastructure and product layers that connect patients and providers at scale.

Our technical stack combines vision models to interpret unstructured healthcare data and build a deep clinical ontology, language and speech models for automated communications, computer-use automation, and LLM based workflows.

Our team brings over a decade of experience from Stripe, Uber, and Bridge, combining deep expertise in ML systems, large-scale infrastructure, and product design.


About the role

Product engineers own all product and technical layers of our stack, acting as hybrid PMs and Eng. You'll take products from inception to full rollout.




About you

 - You care about product craft, for its own sake, and exhibit strong product judgement and taste.

 - Strong software engineering fundamentals. You ship fast and ship well

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Agent Harness
source_jobs.description:
Valerie Health builds the ML infrastructure and product layers that connect patients and providers at scale.

Our technical stack combines vision models to interpret unstructured healthcare data and build a deep clinical ontology, language and speech models for automated communications, computer-use automation, and LLM based workflows.

Our team brings over a decade of experience from Stripe, Uber, and Bridge, combining deep expertise in ML systems, large-scale infrastructure, and product design.



About the role

As a Software Engineer on the Agents team at Valerie, you'll build agent platforms that power complex workflows across computer-vision, patient communication, and computer-use. The work spans building multi-step agent harnesses, sandboxed environments, evaluation frameworks, and agentic infrastructure.



About you

 - You have a deep interest in agentic systems and have built and shipped them to real users in production

 - Strong software engineering fundamentals. You ship fast and ship well

 - Experience building eval datasets or frameworks is a plus

 - Experience with real-time voice systems is a plus

source_jobs.title: Founder's Office
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Valerie Health is the AI front office for independent provider groups. We are reimagining how independent practices operate by using AI to modernize patient/provider communication, enhance care, and drive industry-changing operational efficiencies.

 - Team: We are led by Pete Shalek https://www.linkedin.com/in/petershalek/ (2x health tech exits, including a $600M acquisition by Optum) and Nitin Joshi https://www.linkedin.com/in/njoshi023/ (scaled Uber Eats + Health, Stripe Connect, and Bridge, acquired by Stripe for $1.1B). Beyond our founders, our team brings DNA from category leaders (Uber, Stripe, Meta), high-growth unicorns, and top consulting firms.

 - Trajectory: We partner with leading independent provider groups across the U.S., automating hundreds of thousands of tasks each month. Scaling at 7x year-over-year with 300% NRR as customers consistently expand usage.

 - Funding: We have raised $39M (Series A) from world-class investors including Redpoint Ventures, General Catalyst, Primary Ventures, and BoxGroup.




ABOUT THE ROLE

The Founder’s Office will operate as a force multiplier across Valerie Health, partnering closely with the founders and leadership team on the company’s most important strategic and operational initiatives.

This role reports directly to the CEO and is designed for high-agency operators who want broad scope, high ownership, and direct exposure to company-building at a rapidly scaling AI-native startup.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Partner with Founders and Leadership. Work directly with the founders and leadership team on company strategy, operational scaling, product direction, hiring, and execution against Valerie’s highest-priority initiatives.

 - Lead Critical Projects. Run point on key strategic initiatives, getting them from 0 → 1. This will include standing up critical internal efforts, including our partnership efforts.

 - Support Go-to-Market. Work closely with leadership on customer and GTM initiatives, including strategic customer conversations, operational execution, expansion opportunities, and partnership discussions..

 - Scale the Company Operating System: Support the company’s planning and execution cadence, including leadership reviews, strategic planning, all hands, board preparation, internal communications, and cross-functional coordination.

 - Operate Across the Company. Partner closely with leaders and teams across the organization, helping drive alignment, context sharing, and culture.


ABOUT YOU

Experience:

 - 3–6+ years of experience in high-performance environments (e.g. banking, consulting, investing), including 2+ years at a high-growth startup or VC firm.

 - Founder experience, experience as an early employee, or a strong aspiration to build a company is a significant plus.

 - Track record of leading multiple complex cross-functional initiatives simultaneously from strategy through execution.

 - Strong ability to influence stakeholders, drive alignment across teams, and operate effectively in fast-moving environments.

Attributes:

 - Growth Mindset. Eagerness to learn new approaches, seek feedback, and continually improve skill sets.

 - Strong Communicator. Clear and succinct in both written and verbal communication, capable of adjusting style for different audiences and conveying technical topics to non-technical stakeholders.

 - Incredibly Curious. fast learner, motivated, resourceful

 - Action Oriented. bias to getting things done and making decisions with imperfect information.

 - Self-Starter & Adaptable. Proactively own projects from inception to completion, identify challenges, and offer solutions, thriving in a dynamic work environment.

 - Thrive in Ambiguity. Can work in an environment that is oftentimes unstructured and unpredictable.


COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Be a founding operator with meaningful ownership in an AI-native, high-growth startup

 - Competitive salary, equity and benefits including medical, dental, vision

source_jobs.title: Lead Growth Marketer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Valerie Health is the AI front office for independent provider groups. We are reimagining how independent practices operate by using AI to modernize patient/provider communication, enhance care, and drive industry-changing operational efficiencies.

 - Team: We are led by Pete Shalek https://www.linkedin.com/in/petershalek/ (2x health tech exits, including a $600M acquisition by Optum) and Nitin Joshi https://www.linkedin.com/in/njoshi023/ (scaled Uber Eats + Health, Stripe Connect, and Bridge, acquired by Stripe for $1.1B). Beyond our founders, our team brings DNA from category leaders (Uber, Stripe, Meta), high-growth unicorns, and top consulting firms.

 - Trajectory: We partner with leading independent provider groups across the U.S., automating hundreds of thousands of tasks each month. Scaling at 7x year-over-year with 300% NRR as customers consistently expand usage.

 - Funding: We have raised $39M (Series A) from world-class investors including Redpoint Ventures, General Catalyst, Primary Ventures, and BoxGroup.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Valerie is in a hypergrowth phase, and we are building the marketing function from the ground up. You will be one of the first hires, working with high autonomy and responsibility. When people hear what Valerie does and use our products, they love it. The challenge isn't persuasion, it's reach. This role exists to solve that.

We need a Senior Growth Marketer to own lead generation and pipeline growth. You will generate qualified leads through every channel at your disposal — SEO/GEO, the website, email and LinkedIn campaigns, webinars, conferences, advertising, and more — and turn those into a repeatable engine that feeds our sales team.

This is not a role where you inherit a playbook. You will figure out what works, double down on it, and move fast. Some channels are already producing: conferences, webinars, and LinkedIn are converting well. Others need to be built from scratch. You will work closely with the Head of Marketing, our sales team, and external agencies.

What matters most for this role is how you think and work. You are analytically rigorous: you structure problems, use data to make decisions, and build systems that scale. You are relentlessly resourceful: when something doesn't work, you don't flag it, you fix it. You are creative about solving growth problems, and you move fast enough that learning happens through doing, not planning. Essentially, you get sh*t done. Prior marketing titles matter less than whether you can look at a challenge, figure out how to crack it, and execute.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own Lead Generation. Drive qualified leads across all channels — SEO/GEO, website, email, LinkedIn, webinars, conferences, and paid advertising — and continuously optimize conversion at every stage of the funnel.

 - Campaigns. Run email and LinkedIn campaigns that drive meetings and pipeline. Design the sequences, write the copy, measure the results.

 - Webinars. Plan and execute webinars as a lead generation channel, from topic selection to promotion to post-event follow-up.

 - Conferences. Manage conference strategy alongside the Field Marketing Manager, with responsibility for pre-event outreach and ROI analysis.

 - Paid Advertising. Work with our external partners or manage directly on paid campaigns, A/B message testing, and performance optimization.

 - Reporting. Build the dashboards that let us understand what's generating pipeline and what's not.

 - GTM Systems. Help build the workflows and AI-powered automations that let a small team operate at the pace of a much larger one.
   
   


ABOUT YOU

You might come from growth marketing, demand gen, revenue marketing, or a generalist role at a high-growth startup. You might have started in sales, consulting, or ops and moved into marketing because you're drawn to the problem-solving side of driving growth. Your background matters less than how you think and work.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3

notes: Healthcare AI startup with a mix of tech and non-tech roles
